content: Training Guides & ResourcesBrowse all 45+ training articles and guides — completely freeProfessional Development(8)IntermediateThe Leveson Review: Impact on Police Station Representatives and Defence SolicitorsPart 2 of the Leveson Review makes specific recommendations about police station work — including remote legal advice, the duty solicitor crisis, and rebuilding the defence workforce. This article examines what it means for police station representatives and criminal defence solicitors.10IntermediateThe Leveson Criminal Courts Review: What It Means for the Justice SystemSir Brian Leveson's two-part Independent Review of the Criminal Courts proposes sweeping reforms to a system he says is 'on the brink of collapse'. This article explains the full picture — from the 80,000 case Crown Court backlog to the 135 recommendations for change.10AdvancedProfessional Development for Police Station Representatives: Enhancing Your Career and ExpertiseExplore the importance of continuous professional development, specialization, and strategic career growth for police station representatives.20AdvancedProfessional Development: CPD, Specialization and Career GrowthBuild a sustainable career: CPD requirements and sources, specialization strategies, career progression paths (solicitor, duty solicitor, partnership), advanced skills development, quality assurance, and avoiding burnout in police station work.10AdvancedGetting a Training Contract After PSRAS AccreditationComplete guide to securing a training contract after PSRAS accreditation. Target firms, application strategy, interview prep, SQE route, and alternative pathways to qualification.00AdvancedBuilding Your Career as a Police Station RepLong-term career strategy: getting more work, building reputation, networking, specializing, and growing your police station practice.00AdvancedBuilding Your Rep Business: Getting Regular WorkPractical strategies for building relationships with law firms, getting regular call-outs, and establishing yourself as a go-to representative.00AdvancedStation Intelligence: What Firms Want to KnowBuild valuable station intelligence: custody procedures, helpful staff, phone numbers, parking, and insider knowledge that makes you indispensable to firms.00Dealing with Police(5)AdvancedSeized Property: A Comprehensive Guide to Getting Your Belongings ReturnedComplete legal guide covering police powers to seize property, retention timeframes, court remedies, and step-by-step processes for getting belongings returned after arrest or investigation.10IntermediateWhat Is a Police Warrant in England and Wales? A Comprehensive Guide for ClientsEssential guide for police station representatives on advising clients about police warrants in England & Wales. Covers search warrants, arrest warrants, execution procedures, digital seizure, rights, and legal challenges.10IntermediateNavigating the Police Complaint System for ClientsEssential guide for police station representatives on advising clients through the police complaints process in England & Wales, covering rights, procedures, timelines, and practical advocacy strategies.60IntermediateDealing with Police: Professional Relationships and Managing Custody OfficersNavigate professional relationships with police while protecting clients: understanding custody officer roles, requesting disclosure, challenging PACE breaches, negotiating bail conditions, and managing difficult situations professionally.20IntermediateDealing with Police: Rights, Powers and Practical StrategiesComprehensive guide to dealing with police at stations: understanding police powers, detention time limits, your rights as representative, challenging improper conduct, managing difficult officers, identification procedures, and complaints processes. Includes tactical strategies and intervention templates.00Client Management(3)IntermediateClient Care and Management: Complete GuideComprehensive guide to client care obligations, first impressions, managing difficult behaviors, vulnerable clients, professional conduct standards, and building trust at police stations.100AdvancedBuilding Your Reputation: Client Retention SecretsMaster client retention to build a stable, growing freelance practice. Learn what keeps firms coming back again and again.00AdvancedClient Management: Difficult Clients and Awkward SituationsHow to manage challenging clients: the hostile, the delusional, the time-wasters, and those who won't listen to advice.10Claiming & Billing(4)IntermediateLegal Aid Claiming and Billing: Complete Guide for Police Station Work (Updated February 2026)Updated February 2026: Complete guide to legal aid claiming and billing for police station work. Covers the new harmonised £320 fixed fee, £650 escape threshold (SI 2025/1251), corrected hourly rates from Schedule 4, and the new SaBC claims system launched 4 February 2026.60IntermediateMaximizing Your Legal Aid Claims00IntermediateUnderstanding Legal Aid: Eligibility, Application & Common IssuesComplete guide to criminal legal aid: who qualifies, how to apply, means testing, and solving common problems with the LAA.00IntermediateClaiming Your Fees: Police Station Work Rates & Forms (Updated February 2026)Updated February 2026: Complete guide to claiming police station fees under the new harmonised £320 fixed fee and £650 escape threshold (SI 2025/1251, in force 22 Dec 2025). Includes SaBC billing system changes.00Common Problems(7)IntermediateCommon Problems at Police Stations & How to Solve ThemPractical solutions to 15 common police station problems: dealing with difficult officers, PACE breaches, disclosure refusals, equipment failures, cultural barriers, and professional conflicts. Includes intervention scripts and escalation strategies.20IntermediateTop 10 Catastrophic Fails at Police Stations (And How to Avoid Them)Learn from the 10 worst mistakes reps make at police stations and how to avoid career-ending disasters. Real examples, detailed prevention strategies.10IntermediateDealing With Difficult Custody StaffHandle difficult custody staff professionally while protecting your client's rights. Master assertiveness without aggression.00IntermediateReleased Under Investigation vs Police Bail: Key DifferencesUnderstanding the difference between Release Under Investigation (RUI) and police bail, including timescales, conditions, and what each means for your case.10IntermediateNo Further Action After Police Interview: What Happens NextWhat 'No Further Action' means after a police interview, possible outcomes including summons, cautions, and NFA, plus timescales and what to expect.00IntermediatePolice Cautions in England: Understanding the Types and ConsequencesComplete guide to police cautions in England: simple vs conditional cautions, how long they stay on your record, and the impact on employment, travel, and DBS checks.00IntermediateCommon Offenses: Quick Advice GuideQuick reference guide for advising on the most common police station offenses: assault, theft, drugs, public order, and driving offenses.10Interview Techniques(4)IntermediateMastering Police Interview Techniques: Advanced StrategiesAdvanced guide to managing police interviews: PEACE model, intervention techniques, tactical decision-making, managing difficult scenarios, special category suspects, and professional conduct. Includes practical scripts and case-specific strategies.10IntermediateMastering the No Comment Interview StrategyMaster when and how to advise no comment interviews. Avoid s.34 pitfalls, handle police pressure, and protect your client's position.00IntermediateNo Comment Interviews: When and HowMaster the art of advising clients on no comment interviews. Understand Section 34, prepared statements, and tactical silence.00AdvancedInterview Techniques That Actually WorkAdvanced strategies for managing police interviews: tactical silences, prepared statements, handling special warnings, and protecting vulnerable clients.00At The Station(10)IntermediateYour First Police Station Attendance: Step-by-Step GuideComplete step-by-step guide to your first police station attendance: preparation, arrival procedures, custody record review, disclosure requests, client consultation, interview management, and post-attendance steps. Includes practical tips and common mistakes to avoid.00IntermediateReading Police Disclosure: What To Look ForLearn how to analyze police disclosure, spot weaknesses in their case, and make informed decisions about interview strategy.00IntermediateTop 10 Tips for Police Station RepresentativesEssential practical tips from experienced reps - use case management packages effectively, get full firm details, examine custody records minutely, get comprehensive disclosure from IO, and use technology to deliver professional service every time.00BeginnerThe Police Caution Explained: Your Rights When ArrestedComplete guide to the police caution for clients and police station representatives. Understand your right to silence, adverse inferences, interview strategies, vulnerable clients, and how to protect yourself when arrested. Fully referenced with 73 footnotes.30IntermediateVoluntary Police Interviews: What You Need to KnowComplete guide to voluntary police interviews - what they are, what happens, your rights to free legal advice, and why you should never attend alone.00IntermediatePACE Codes Quick Reference: A-H SummaryEssential quick reference to all PACE codes: what each one covers and the key rights for your clients.00IntermediateYouth Justice: Representing Under 18s at Police StationsSpecialist guide to representing juveniles: additional rights, appropriate adult role, youth court procedures, and enhanced safeguards.00IntermediateYouth Suspects: Special Procedures and ProtectionsEverything you need to know about representing juveniles: appropriate adult requirements, special procedures, and enhanced protections under PACE.00IntermediateMental Health and Vulnerability in CustodyRecognizing and supporting clients with mental health issues, learning disabilities, and other vulnerabilities in police custody.20IntermediatePACE Code C: Custody and Interview RightsEssential PACE Code C rules every rep must know: detention time limits, interview procedures, breaks, and vulnerable detainee provisions.00Getting Started(4)BeginnerGetting Started as a Police Station RepresentativeComplete beginners' guide to starting work as a police station representative: qualification pathways, employment options, essential skills, first-day guidance, and professional development. Includes PACE foundations, legal aid system, and career progression routes.00BeginnerYour First Police Station Call Out - Complete GuideStep-by-step guide for your first ever police station attendance. What to bring, what to do, what to say, and how to handle the whole process professionally from start to finish.10BeginnerUnderstanding Duty Solicitors: Your Free Legal LifelineEverything you need to know about duty solicitors at police stations and courts - who they are, what they do, common myths, and why you should use them.00BeginnerVoluntary Police Interviews: Your Complete GuideEssential guide to voluntary police interviews: what they are, why you need a solicitor, what happens during the interview, and your rights.00

This article examines what it means for police station representatives and criminal defence solicitors.10
